user.title: Configure Remote Boot Manager
user.description:
If you need help with the configuration have a look here: {documentation_url}

A secure, random Webhook ID will be automatically generated for you in the next step to protect your system from unauthorized access.

webhook_info.title: Generated Webhook ID
webhook_info.description:
Here is your securely generated Webhook ID. **Please copy this now as it will not be shown again.**

```text
{webhook_id}
```

For full instructions on configuring and installing the agent, visit the [Remote Boot Agent]({agent_url}) repository.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Remote Boot Manager
reconfigure.description: **Warning:** Regenerating the webhook ID will break the connection with your existing agents until they are updated with the new ID.

reconfigure_webhook_info.title: New Webhook ID
reconfigure_webhook_info.description:
Here is your securely generated new Webhook ID. **Please copy this now as it will not be shown again.**

```text
{webhook_id}
```

Remember to update all your Remote Boot Agents with this new ID.

For full instructions on configuring and installing the agent, visit the [Remote Boot Agent]({agent_url}) repository.

host_config.title: Configure {host_name}
host_config.description:
Select a script to gracefully shut down the remote host. 

**Note:** You can also manually adjust the network addresses below for debugging purposes, but these will be automatically overwritten the next time the Remote Boot Agent checks in!

data_description.turn_off_script: Path to a script that will be executed when the agent checks in. This script should gracefully shut down the remote host. If left blank, the host will simply be marked as 'off' without executing any commands.
data_description.address: The network address that HA will ping to check to see if the host is online. This can be an IP address or hostname. If left blank, there will not be any ping checks.
data_description.broadcast_address: The IP address to send magic packets to. Defaults to `255.255.255.255` (`ff02::1` for ipv6) and is typically not changed.
data_description.broadcast_port: The port to send magic packets to. Defaults to `9` and is typically not changed.

send_magic_packet.name: Send magic packet
send_magic_packet.description: Send a magic packet to wake up a device with "Wake-On-LAN".

mac.name: [%key:common::config_flow::data::mac%]
mac.description: MAC address of the device to wake up.

broadcast_address.name: Broadcast IP address
broadcast_address.description: IP address of the host to send the magic packet to.

broadcast_port.name: Broadcast port
broadcast_port.description: Port to send the magic packet to.
